caption: Edwardian postcard: woman fends off the stork with an umbrella. "And the Villain Still Pursues Her."
Salvador Dalí walks his anteater through Paris, 1969.
Victorian Australian Christmas card: terrified child cornered by an emu. "An Unwelcome Greeting."
Audrey Hepburn shops with her pet fawn Pippin, 1958.
Man atop a towering penny-farthing steals a kiss from a second-floor window.
1970s vision of the office of the future, complete with space-helmet headset.
A frog in a tutu juggles daggers and cannonballs on a tightrope. Sewing machine sold separately.
Victorian Alpine climbers — one in full skirts — cross a crevasse on a single plank, c. 1900.
Frankenstein's monster meets a mermaid between Universal Pictures sets, 1948.
Suffragette Lady Florence Norman rides her motor-scooter to work, 1916.
Margaret Gibbs kissed by her fiancé; conjoined sister Mary looks on, 1940s.
Victorian Christmas card: a mouse rides a lobster bearing seasonal greetings.
Boys in astronaut suits with Space Hoppers, Glasgow back court, 1970s.
Young man sits on a paper-moon carnival prop beneath a sign: NOBODY LOVES ME.
"The Birthday Cake" — two dressed kittens await cake. Harry Whittier Frees, 1914.
Gene Simmons sits with face-painted fans, platform boots filling the foreground, 1970s.
The Godzilla suit holds an umbrella for actress Momoko Kôchi on set, 1954.
A kitten, in a coat, headphones on, asleep beside a radio set, 1926.
Alma Hitchcock, cheerfully retrieving dinner beside Alfred's wax head, 1970s
Child dressed in ape mask sits on porch steps, Kokomo, IN, c. 1950
Snakepit operator holds sign: "WE DO NOT HAVE A DINOSAUR" — Hwy 66, Oklahoma, 1973
A cat hangs tame rats in socks on the washing line to dry, 1933
Norman Rockwell's Saturday Evening Post cover: couple consults a Ouija board, 1920
Auxiliary Fire Service couple kiss under mistletoe in gas masks, Christmas 1940
Woman stands in a full-body costume made entirely of leaves, late Victorian/Edwardian
Knife-throwing target girl, arms crossed, knives pinned around her, 19th century
Signor Martino levitates Mademoiselle Nita on stage, 1900.
Mid-19th-century crinolines too wide for the London omnibus.
Jayne Mansfield rides a baby elephant at her own birthday party, 1960s.
Deep-sea diver shelters from the rain with an umbrella, 1949.
Three women dine at table while alligators circle — Luna Park Farm, 1930s.
Two children in box and jack-o'-lantern masks stand in a muddy yard.
Veronica Lake demonstrates why war workers shouldn't wear peek-a-boo hair, 1943.
The Lavery Electric Automatic Phrenometer measures a girl's skull, 1907.
A medium emits a glowing rod of ectoplasm at a Spiritualist séance, c. 1900s.
Martin Witter, 10, watches his snail team compete in a chariot race.
Boy stands inside a giant laughing clown mouth — funhouse entrance, Santa Cruz, 1953.
A dapper man in a bowler hat makes a home for birds in his beard, 1940.
Two white-faced clowns in striped suits play stringed instruments, a ball between them, c. 1940s.
Albert Einstein holds a marionette of himself after watching it perform, Los Angeles.
